response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine language-tooling interest — they built a D test runner, an assertion library, and worked on the NI LabWindows/CVI IDE itself — but the core stack requirements are largely unmet. There is no evidence of Kotlin or Spring experience, Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, device orchestration), and no deep JVM expertise is demonstrated. For a role centered on enterprise Java/Kotlin frameworks and JVM internals, the gap is too wide despite the relevant IDE-adjacent background.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Authored developer tooling: test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), web server libs
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, community teaching and workshops
concerns: No Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience; Java exposure is minimal and dated
No demonstrated deep JVM knowledge (career centers on TypeScript/Node and DLang)
Recent work is full-stack web/AI tooling, not enterprise frameworks or application servers

key_factors.factor: Broad polyglot experience but shallow in required stack
